params.text: Patient is a 45-year-old female admitted for inpatient care. Active problems include hypertension (I10), medication use (Z79.899), and dependence on care (Z74.09). Vital signs are stable: BP 130/70, HR 70, SpO2 96%. Labs reviewed: creatinine 1.1 (within normal limits), troponin normal. No pending critical lab results identified at this time. No adverse events or inbox messages. Plan: continue current management, monitor vitals, and reassess for discharge readiness. Discharge planning to be initiated pending clinical stability and resolution of any outstanding care needs.

params.plan_text: Discharge plan for patient-v2-throughput-bgpm-000 (45F): Patient is clinically stable with normal vital signs and labs. Discharge to be considered once care team confirms all outstanding needs are resolved. Instructions: (1) Continue prescribed medications as directed. (2) Follow up with primary care physician within 1 week of discharge. (3) Monitor blood pressure at home; seek care if BP exceeds 160/100 or symptoms worsen. (4) Return to ED if experiencing chest pain, shortness of breath, or other concerning symptoms. (5) Ensure adequate support at home given documented care dependence (Z74.09). Discharge is contingent on care team sign-off and confirmation that no unresolved safety concerns remain.
